docs: Videndum market intelligence + obsolescence risk report

  - 8 competitors with positioning (DJI, SmallRig, Tilta, Hollyland, etc.)

OBSOLESCENCE_REPORT.md — DB cross-analysis (videndum_full_context vs market trends):
  - 9 SKUs with >84% revenue decline 2022→2024 (AB8675*, AB10* battery families)
  - 1 product with negative net intake (returns > orders — ABS2051-000101)
  - Top 5 healthy SKUs with active order book + pipeline
  - Book-to-Bill Oct 2025: 0.87 (consuming backlog)
  - Inventory data gap documented with corrective SQL

commit.message:
Add Unreal Engine plugin with threaded and async planning

Introduces GOAPie_UE, an Unreal Engine plugin that wraps the core
GOAPie library with UE threading support:

- FGOAPiePlannerTask: FRunnable-based task that executes planning on a
  background thread with game-thread completion delegates
- FGOAPieAsyncPlanner: High-level manager for concurrent plan requests
  with handle-based cancellation and automatic cleanup
- GOAPieTypes.h: UE-friendly namespace aliases for core types
- GOAPieExample.h: Comprehensive usage examples (not compiled)

Threading is entirely in game-code-level infrastructure — the core
GOAPie library remains synchronous and untouched. Supports both
synchronous and async planning modes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Initial commit: kinematic indicator nest FreeCAD script

Exact-constraint kinematic coupling (3x ball/two-pin cradle, 35mm BC,
120 deg spacing) with Hall effect crash detection for manual assembly
station operator feedback.

Phase 1: passive indicator (hall + comparator + LED)
Phase 2: lockable precision nest (electromagnet coil, groove pre-machined)

Generates base plate, three nest plate variants (blank, pin location,
ledge location), coupled/exploded assemblies, and STL exports.

commit.message:
Unstable Devices activation-time trigger + Power Converter combat-time trigger

Unstable Devices (Saska Teft): moved from incorrect round-start to activation-time.
Free once-per-activation button in thread; auto-confirm if 1 friendly in LOS,
picker if multiple. Grants 1 Device token to chosen friendly.

Power Converter (Saska Teft): moved from activation-start auto-discard to
combat-time hand-channel prompt. When a friendly with a Device token attacks,
pings Saska's player in hand channel to approve. On approve: die picker →
optional color swap → reroll. Once per round. Device token NOT consumed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add device identification: power-cycle MAC detection + OUI candidate matching

# 2026-03-10: Two approaches to resolve Alexa plugs/lights to network MACs:
#
# 1. "Identify" button — turns device off for 10s, snapshots Deco client
#    list before/after, reports which MAC(s) dropped offline. Works best
#    for devices that lose WiFi when powered off (things plugged INTO a
#    smart plug). Smart plugs themselves stay on WiFi even when "off".
#
# 2. "MACs" button — OUI-based candidate matching. Maps Amazon device
#    serial prefixes to known manufacturer OUI ranges:
#    - Amazon Smart Plugs -> 90:39:5f (4 candidates)
#    - Amazon Basics bulbs -> Espressif 10:06:1c, d4:d4:da (8 candidates)
#    - Amazon branded lights -> 68:13:f3, 24:ce:33 (2 candidates)
#
# Backend: POST /alexa/smart-home/{id}/identify, GET /smart-home/candidates
# Frontend: Identify button with results, MACs button with candidate list

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: close TASK-013 — PM approved, GA4 + Search Console live

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Monte Carlo simulation with standardized Student-t shocks

Raw Student-t(nu~3) draws have variance nu/(nu-2), causing explosive
simulation paths. Standardize shocks to unit variance to match the
GARCH model's internal assumption. Also improve IGARCH detection
for the near-unit-root case (alpha+beta ~ 1).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

  - Top 20 parts by order_book + opportunities (weighted pipeline)

  - ComposedChart: Revenue bars vs Order Intake bars + B2B line (Recharts)

  - Skeleton loading + error state with AlertTriangle icon

New types: MonthlyIntakeRow, PipelineRow, AnalyticsKPIs, AnalyticsData

commit.message:
remove LFS binaries, add download-deps.sh for air-gapped prep

Binary files (RPMs, tarballs) no longer tracked in git — they were LFS
pointers that broke ZIP downloads. Users now run scripts/download-deps.sh
on a connected machine to fetch everything into downloads/, then transfer
the full directory to the air-gapped target.

- Remove all LFS tracking from .gitattributes
- Add .gitignore excluding downloads/*.rpm, *.tar, *.tar.gz
- Add scripts/download-deps.sh with --skip-images/rpms/client flags
- Update README to reference download-deps.sh instead of manual curls
- Dashboard JSON files remain committed (small text files)

feat: rewrite investec_scanner to use EWS/NTLM (HexOnline Exchange)

IMAP times out on HexOnline on-premise Exchange server.
EWS (Exchange Web Services) with NTLM auth confirmed working for both
Courtney@ and mary@ (HTTP 200, inbox readable).

Changes:
- Replace imaplib with requests + requests_ntlm
- FindItem SOAP request with date restriction (last 25h)
- Parse XML response for Subject, From, Body
- Graceful handling of HexOnline internal CA (verify=False)
- Add requests_ntlm>=1.2.0 to requirements.txt
- EWS_URL / EWS_USER_1 / EWS_PASS_1 / EWS_USER_2 / EWS_PASS_2 set on Render

commit.message:
Add Smart Home Control page with cookie-based plug/light toggle

# 2026-03-10: New "Smart Home" tab to toggle all Alexa-linked plugs and
# lights on/off, so you can identify which physical device each one is.
# Uses the phoenix/state cookie API (not OAuth directives, which need
# alexa::smart_home scope we don't have).
#
# Backend: GET /alexa/smart-home/devices, POST /alexa/smart-home/{id}/control
# Frontend: SmartHomePage with ON/OFF buttons + brightness presets for lights
# Client: alexa_client.smart_home_control() via PUT /api/phoenix/state
